{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster |native_name =大村横穴群 |alternate_name = |image =Omura Yokoana Group, higashi-1.jpg |alt = |caption = Omura Yokoana Group | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates = {{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E|region:JP-45|display=inline}} | location = [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis |part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = |built = 6th-7th century CE |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster'''|大村横穴群|Ōmura yokoana-gun}} is a cluster of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs dug in artificial caves in a [[tuff]] cliff of located in the Shiromoto-machi neighborhood of the city of [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto|Hitoyoshi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139857 |title=大村横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Background== The Ōmura Cave Tombs are located over a 800-meter stretch from east-to-west on the cliffs on the south side of the Murayama Plateau on the right bank of the [[Kuma River (Japan)|Kuma River]], which runs through the south of the prefecture. These tombs were built during the Kofun period in the 6th and 7th centuries. The [[burial chamber]]s are square with rounded corners and two or three sections for the corpse bed. There are 27 tombs in the group, eight of which are [[decorated kofun]], with relief carings or paintings of animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.) on the outside. The entrance gates are square or dome-shaped, and many have decorative borders. The inner wall of the chamber of cave 15 has five double circular lines engraved on it, while the outer wall of the gate has animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.). In particular, cave 7 has eight triangular patterns arranged in a sawtooth pattern on the upper part of the outer wall, and in the upper right corner, a double triangular pattern is engraved on it, and on the right side, a kite-shaped quiver is drawn with ten arrows. Furthermore, five horses are engraved on the outer wall on the left, which is noteworthy for its dynamic and three-dimensional depiction. In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated [[grave goods]] include a straight sword and [[Sue ware]] pottery. The site is about a five-minute walk from [[Hitoyoshi Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hisatsu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster |native_name =大村横穴群 |alternate_name = |image =Omura Yokoana Group, higashi-1.jpg |alt = |caption = Omura Yokoana Group | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates = {{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E|region:JP-45|display=inline}} | location = [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis |part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = |built = 6th-7th century CE |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster'''|大村横穴群|Ōmura yokoana-gun}} is a cluster of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs dug in artificial caves in a [[tuff]] cliff of located in the Shiromoto-machi neighborhood of the city of [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto|Hitoyoshi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139857 |title=大村横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Background== The Ōmura Cave Tombs are located over a 800-meter stretch from east-to-west on the cliffs on the south side of the Murayama Plateau on the right bank of the [[Kuma River (Japan)|Kuma River]], which runs through the south of the prefecture. These tombs were built during the Kofun period in the 6th and 7th centuries. The [[burial chamber]]s are square with rounded corners and two or three sections for the corpse bed. There are 27 tombs in the group, eight of which are [[decorated kofun]], with relief carings or paintings of animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.) on the outside. The entrance gates are square or dome-shaped, and many have decorative borders. The inner wall of the chamber of cave 15 has five double circular lines engraved on it, while the outer wall of the gate has animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.). In particular, cave 7 has eight triangular patterns arranged in a sawtooth pattern on the upper part of the outer wall, and in the upper right corner, a double triangular pattern is engraved on it, and on the right side, a kite-shaped quiver is drawn with ten arrows. Furthermore, five horses are engraved on the outer wall on the left, which is noteworthy for its dynamic and three-dimensional depiction. In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated [[grave goods]] include a straight sword and [[Sue ware]] pottery. The site is about a five-minute walk from [[Hitoyoshi Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hisatsu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster |native_name =大村横穴群 |alternate_name = |image =Omura Yokoana Group, higashi-1.jpg |alt = |caption = Omura Yokoana Group | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates = {{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E|region:JP-45|display=inline}} | location = [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis |part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = |built = 6th-7th century CE |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster'''|大村横穴群|Ōmura yokoana-gun}} is a cluster of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs dug in artificial caves in a [[tuff]] cliff of located in the Shiromoto-machi neighborhood of the city of [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto|Hitoyoshi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139857 |title=大村横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Background== The Ōmura Cave Tombs are located over a 800-meter stretch from east-to-west on the cliffs on the south side of the Murayama Plateau on the right bank of the [[Kuma River (Japan)|Kuma River]], which runs through the south of the prefecture. These tombs were built during the Kofun period in the 6th and 7th centuries. The [[burial chamber]]s are square with rounded corners and two or three sections for the corpse bed. There are 27 tombs in the group, eight of which are [[decorated kofun]], with relief carings or paintings of animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.) on the outside. The entrance gates are square or dome-shaped, and many have decorative borders. The inner wall of the chamber of cave 15 has five double circular lines engraved on it, while the outer wall of the gate has animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.). In particular, cave 7 has eight triangular patterns arranged in a sawtooth pattern on the upper part of the outer wall, and in the upper right corner, a double triangular pattern is engraved on it, and on the right side, a kite-shaped quiver is drawn with ten arrows. Furthermore, five horses are engraved on the outer wall on the left, which is noteworthy for its dynamic and three-dimensional depiction. In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated [[grave goods]] include a straight sword and [[Sue ware]] pottery. The site is about a five-minute walk from [[Hitoyoshi Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hisatsu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster |native_name =大村横穴群 |alternate_name = |image =Omura Yokoana Group, higashi-1.jpg |alt = |caption = Omura Yokoana Group | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates = {{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E|region:JP-45|display=inline}} | location = [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis |part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = |built = 6th-7th century CE |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster'''|大村横穴群|Ōmura yokoana-gun}} is a cluster of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs dug in artificial caves in a [[tuff]] cliff of located in the Shiromoto-machi neighborhood of the city of [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto|Hitoyoshi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139857 |title=大村横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Background== The Ōmura Cave Tombs are located over a 800-meter stretch from east-to-west on the cliffs on the south side of the Murayama Plateau on the right bank of the [[Kuma River (Japan)|Kuma River]], which runs through the south of the prefecture. These tombs were built during the Kofun period in the 6th and 7th centuries. The [[burial chamber]]s are square with rounded corners and two or three sections for the corpse bed. There are 27 tombs in the group, eight of which are [[decorated kofun]], with relief carings or paintings of animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.) on the outside. The entrance gates are square or dome-shaped, and many have decorative borders. The inner wall of the chamber of cave 15 has five double circular lines engraved on it, while the outer wall of the gate has animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.). In particular, cave 7 has eight triangular patterns arranged in a sawtooth pattern on the upper part of the outer wall, and in the upper right corner, a double triangular pattern is engraved on it, and on the right side, a kite-shaped quiver is drawn with ten arrows. Furthermore, five horses are engraved on the outer wall on the left, which is noteworthy for its dynamic and three-dimensional depiction. In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated [[grave goods]] include a straight sword and [[Sue ware]] pottery. The site is about a five-minute walk from [[Hitoyoshi Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hisatsu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Omura Yokoana Group}} *[https://hitoyoshikuma-guide.com/2019/03/03/oomurayokoanagun/ Hitoyoshi-Kuma Guide]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster |native_name =大村横穴群 |alternate_name = |image =Omura Yokoana Group, higashi-1.jpg |alt = |caption = Omura Yokoana Group | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates = {{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E|region:JP-45|display=inline}} | location = [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis |part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = |built = 6th-7th century CE |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster'''|大村横穴群|Ōmura yokoana-gun}} is a cluster of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs dug in artificial caves in a [[tuff]] cliff of located in the Shiromoto-machi neighborhood of the city of [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto|Hitoyoshi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139857 |title=大村横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Background== The Ōmura Cave Tombs are located over a 800-meter stretch from east-to-west on the cliffs on the south side of the Murayama Plateau on the right bank of the [[Kuma River (Japan)|Kuma River]], which runs through the south of the prefecture. These tombs were built during the Kofun period in the 6th and 7th centuries. The [[burial chamber]]s are square with rounded corners and two or three sections for the corpse bed. There are 27 tombs in the group, eight of which are [[decorated kofun]], with relief carings or paintings of animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.) on the outside. The entrance gates are square or dome-shaped, and many have decorative borders. The inner wall of the chamber of cave 15 has five double circular lines engraved on it, while the outer wall of the gate has animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.). In particular, cave 7 has eight triangular patterns arranged in a sawtooth pattern on the upper part of the outer wall, and in the upper right corner, a double triangular pattern is engraved on it, and on the right side, a kite-shaped quiver is drawn with ten arrows. Furthermore, five horses are engraved on the outer wall on the left, which is noteworthy for its dynamic and three-dimensional depiction. In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated [[grave goods]] include a straight sword and [[Sue ware]] pottery. The site is about a five-minute walk from [[Hitoyoshi Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hisatsu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster |native_name =大村横穴群 |alternate_name = |image =Omura Yokoana Group, higashi-1.jpg |alt = |caption = Omura Yokoana Group | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates = {{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E|region:JP-45|display=inline}} | location = [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis |part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = |built = 6th-7th century CE |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster'''|大村横穴群|Ōmura yokoana-gun}} is a cluster of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs dug in artificial caves in a [[tuff]] cliff of located in the Shiromoto-machi neighborhood of the city of [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto|Hitoyoshi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139857 |title=大村横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Background== The Ōmura Cave Tombs are located over a 800-meter stretch from east-to-west on the cliffs on the south side of the Murayama Plateau on the right bank of the [[Kuma River (Japan)|Kuma River]], which runs through the south of the prefecture. These tombs were built during the Kofun period in the 6th and 7th centuries. The [[burial chamber]]s are square with rounded corners and two or three sections for the corpse bed. There are 27 tombs in the group, eight of which are [[decorated kofun]], with relief carings or paintings of animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.) on the outside. The entrance gates are square or dome-shaped, and many have decorative borders. The inner wall of the chamber of cave 15 has five double circular lines engraved on it, while the outer wall of the gate has animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.). In particular, cave 7 has eight triangular patterns arranged in a sawtooth pattern on the upper part of the outer wall, and in the upper right corner, a double triangular pattern is engraved on it, and on the right side, a kite-shaped quiver is drawn with ten arrows. Furthermore, five horses are engraved on the outer wall on the left, which is noteworthy for its dynamic and three-dimensional depiction. In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated [[grave goods]] include a straight sword and [[Sue ware]] pottery. The site is about a five-minute walk from [[Hitoyoshi Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hisatsu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

In particular, cave 7 has eight triangular patterns arranged in a sawtooth pattern on the upper part of the outer wall, and in the upper right corner, a double triangular pattern is engraved on it, and on the right side, a kite-shaped quiver is drawn with ten arrows. Furthermore, five horses are engraved on the outer wall on the left, which is noteworthy for its dynamic and three-dimensional depiction. In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated [[grave goods]] include a straight sword and [[Sue ware]] pottery. The site is about a five-minute walk from [[Hitoyoshi Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hisatsu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster |native_name =大村横穴群 |alternate_name = |image =Omura Yokoana Group, higashi-1.jpg |alt = |caption = Omura Yokoana Group | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates = {{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E|region:JP-45|display=inline}} | location = [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis |part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = |built = 6th-7th century CE |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster'''|大村横穴群|Ōmura yokoana-gun}} is a cluster of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs dug in artificial caves in a [[tuff]] cliff of located in the Shiromoto-machi neighborhood of the city of [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto|Hitoyoshi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139857 |title=大村横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Background== The Ōmura Cave Tombs are located over a 800-meter stretch from east-to-west on the cliffs on the south side of the Murayama Plateau on the right bank of the [[Kuma River (Japan)|Kuma River]], which runs through the south of the prefecture. These tombs were built during the Kofun period in the 6th and 7th centuries. The [[burial chamber]]s are square with rounded corners and two or three sections for the corpse bed. There are 27 tombs in the group, eight of which are [[decorated kofun]], with relief carings or paintings of animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.) on the outside. The entrance gates are square or dome-shaped, and many have decorative borders. The inner wall of the chamber of cave 15 has five double circular lines engraved on it, while the outer wall of the gate has animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.). In particular, cave 7 has eight triangular patterns arranged in a sawtooth pattern on the upper part of the outer wall, and in the upper right corner, a double triangular pattern is engraved on it, and on the right side, a kite-shaped quiver is drawn with ten arrows. Furthermore, five horses are engraved on the outer wall on the left, which is noteworthy for its dynamic and three-dimensional depiction. In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated [[grave goods]] include a straight sword and [[Sue ware]] pottery. The site is about a five-minute walk from [[Hitoyoshi Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hisatsu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster |native_name =大村横穴群 |alternate_name = |image =Omura Yokoana Group, higashi-1.jpg |alt = |caption = Omura Yokoana Group | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates = {{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E|region:JP-45|display=inline}} | location = [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis |part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = |built = 6th-7th century CE |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster'''|大村横穴群|Ōmura yokoana-gun}} is a cluster of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs dug in artificial caves in a [[tuff]] cliff of located in the Shiromoto-machi neighborhood of the city of [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto|Hitoyoshi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139857 |title=大村横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Background== The Ōmura Cave Tombs are located over a 800-meter stretch from east-to-west on the cliffs on the south side of the Murayama Plateau on the right bank of the [[Kuma River (Japan)|Kuma River]], which runs through the south of the prefecture. These tombs were built during the Kofun period in the 6th and 7th centuries. The [[burial chamber]]s are square with rounded corners and two or three sections for the corpse bed. There are 27 tombs in the group, eight of which are [[decorated kofun]], with relief carings or paintings of animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.) on the outside. The entrance gates are square or dome-shaped, and many have decorative borders. The inner wall of the chamber of cave 15 has five double circular lines engraved on it, while the outer wall of the gate has animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.). In particular, cave 7 has eight triangular patterns arranged in a sawtooth pattern on the upper part of the outer wall, and in the upper right corner, a double triangular pattern is engraved on it, and on the right side, a kite-shaped quiver is drawn with ten arrows. Furthermore, five horses are engraved on the outer wall on the left, which is noteworthy for its dynamic and three-dimensional depiction. In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated [[grave goods]] include a straight sword and [[Sue ware]] pottery. The site is about a five-minute walk from [[Hitoyoshi Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hisatsu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster |native_name =大村横穴群 |alternate_name = |image =Omura Yokoana Group, higashi-1.jpg |alt = |caption = Omura Yokoana Group | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates = {{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E|region:JP-45|display=inline}} | location = [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis |part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = |built = 6th-7th century CE |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster'''|大村横穴群|Ōmura yokoana-gun}} is a cluster of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs dug in artificial caves in a [[tuff]] cliff of located in the Shiromoto-machi neighborhood of the city of [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto|Hitoyoshi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139857 |title=大村横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Background== The Ōmura Cave Tombs are located over a 800-meter stretch from east-to-west on the cliffs on the south side of the Murayama Plateau on the right bank of the [[Kuma River (Japan)|Kuma River]], which runs through the south of the prefecture. These tombs were built during the Kofun period in the 6th and 7th centuries. The [[burial chamber]]s are square with rounded corners and two or three sections for the corpse bed. There are 27 tombs in the group, eight of which are [[decorated kofun]], with relief carings or paintings of animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.) on the outside. The entrance gates are square or dome-shaped, and many have decorative borders. The inner wall of the chamber of cave 15 has five double circular lines engraved on it, while the outer wall of the gate has animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.). In particular, cave 7 has eight triangular patterns arranged in a sawtooth pattern on the upper part of the outer wall, and in the upper right corner, a double triangular pattern is engraved on it, and on the right side, a kite-shaped quiver is drawn with ten arrows. Furthermore, five horses are engraved on the outer wall on the left, which is noteworthy for its dynamic and three-dimensional depiction. In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated [[grave goods]] include a straight sword and [[Sue ware]] pottery. The site is about a five-minute walk from [[Hitoyoshi Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hisatsu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster |native_name =大村横穴群 |alternate_name = |image =Omura Yokoana Group, higashi-1.jpg |alt = |caption = Omura Yokoana Group | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates = {{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E|region:JP-45|display=inline}} | location = [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis |part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = |built = 6th-7th century CE |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster'''|大村横穴群|Ōmura yokoana-gun}} is a cluster of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs dug in artificial caves in a [[tuff]] cliff of located in the Shiromoto-machi neighborhood of the city of [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto|Hitoyoshi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139857 |title=大村横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Background== The Ōmura Cave Tombs are located over a 800-meter stretch from east-to-west on the cliffs on the south side of the Murayama Plateau on the right bank of the [[Kuma River (Japan)|Kuma River]], which runs through the south of the prefecture. These tombs were built during the Kofun period in the 6th and 7th centuries. The [[burial chamber]]s are square with rounded corners and two or three sections for the corpse bed. There are 27 tombs in the group, eight of which are [[decorated kofun]], with relief carings or paintings of animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.) on the outside. The entrance gates are square or dome-shaped, and many have decorative borders. The inner wall of the chamber of cave 15 has five double circular lines engraved on it, while the outer wall of the gate has animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.). In particular, cave 7 has eight triangular patterns arranged in a sawtooth pattern on the upper part of the outer wall, and in the upper right corner, a double triangular pattern is engraved on it, and on the right side, a kite-shaped quiver is drawn with ten arrows. Furthermore, five horses are engraved on the outer wall on the left, which is noteworthy for its dynamic and three-dimensional depiction. In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated [[grave goods]] include a straight sword and [[Sue ware]] pottery. The site is about a five-minute walk from [[Hitoyoshi Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hisatsu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster |native_name =大村横穴群 |alternate_name = |image =Omura Yokoana Group, higashi-1.jpg |alt = |caption = Omura Yokoana Group | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates = {{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E|region:JP-45|display=inline}} | location = [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis |part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = |built = 6th-7th century CE |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster'''|大村横穴群|Ōmura yokoana-gun}} is a cluster of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs dug in artificial caves in a [[tuff]] cliff of located in the Shiromoto-machi neighborhood of the city of [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto|Hitoyoshi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139857 |title=大村横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Background== The Ōmura Cave Tombs are located over a 800-meter stretch from east-to-west on the cliffs on the south side of the Murayama Plateau on the right bank of the [[Kuma River (Japan)|Kuma River]], which runs through the south of the prefecture. These tombs were built during the Kofun period in the 6th and 7th centuries. The [[burial chamber]]s are square with rounded corners and two or three sections for the corpse bed. There are 27 tombs in the group, eight of which are [[decorated kofun]], with relief carings or paintings of animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.) on the outside. The entrance gates are square or dome-shaped, and many have decorative borders. The inner wall of the chamber of cave 15 has five double circular lines engraved on it, while the outer wall of the gate has animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.). In particular, cave 7 has eight triangular patterns arranged in a sawtooth pattern on the upper part of the outer wall, and in the upper right corner, a double triangular pattern is engraved on it, and on the right side, a kite-shaped quiver is drawn with ten arrows. Furthermore, five horses are engraved on the outer wall on the left, which is noteworthy for its dynamic and three-dimensional depiction. In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated [[grave goods]] include a straight sword and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. The site is about a five-minute walk from [[Hitoyoshi Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hisatsu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster |native_name =大村横穴群 |alternate_name = |image =Omura Yokoana Group, higashi-1.jpg |alt = |caption = Omura Yokoana Group | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates = {{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E|region:JP-45|display=inline}} | location = [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis | part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = |built = 6th-7th century CE |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster'''|大村横穴群|Ōmura yokoana-gun}} is a cluster of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs dug in artificial caves in a [[tuff]] cliff of located in the Shiromoto-machi neighborhood of the city of [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto|Hitoyoshi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139857 |title=大村横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Background== The Ōmura Cave Tombs are located over a 800-meter stretch from east-to-west on the cliffs on the south side of the Murayama Plateau on the right bank of the [[Kuma River (Japan)|Kuma River]], which runs through the south of the prefecture. These tombs were built during the Kofun period in the 6th and 7th centuries. The [[burial chamber]]s are square with rounded corners and two or three sections for the corpse bed. There are 27 tombs in the group, eight of which are [[decorated kofun]], with relief carings or paintings of animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.) on the outside. The entrance gates are square or dome-shaped, and many have decorative borders. The inner wall of the chamber of cave 15 has five double circular lines engraved on it, while the outer wall of the gate has animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.). In particular, cave 7 has eight triangular patterns arranged in a sawtooth pattern on the upper part of the outer wall, and in the upper right corner, a double triangular pattern is engraved on it, and on the right side, a kite-shaped quiver is drawn with ten arrows. Furthermore, five horses are engraved on the outer wall on the left, which is noteworthy for its dynamic and three-dimensional depiction. In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated [[grave goods]] include a straight sword and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. The site is about a five-minute walk from [[Hitoyoshi Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hisatsu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated [[grave goods]] include a straight sword and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. The site is about a five-minute walk from [[Hitoyoshi Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hisatsu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster |native_name =大村横穴群 |alternate_name = |image =Omura Yokoana Group, higashi-1.jpg |alt = |caption = Omura Yokoana Group | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates = {{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E|region:JP-45|display=inline}} | location = [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis | part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = |built = 6th-7th century CE |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster'''|大村横穴群|Ōmura yokoana-gun}} is a cluster of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs dug in artificial caves in a [[tuff]] cliff of located in the Shiromoto-machi neighborhood of the city of [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto|Hitoyoshi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139857 |title=大村横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Background== The Ōmura Cave Tombs are located over a 800-meter stretch from east-to-west on the cliffs on the south side of the Murayama Plateau on the right bank of the [[Kuma River (Japan)|Kuma River]], which runs through the south of the prefecture. These tombs were built during the Kofun period in the 6th and 7th centuries. The [[burial chamber]]s are square with rounded corners and two or three sections for the corpse bed. There are 27 tombs in the group, eight of which are [[decorated kofun]], with relief carings or paintings of animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.) on the outside. The entrance gates are square or dome-shaped, and many have decorative borders. The inner wall of the chamber of cave 15 has five double circular lines engraved on it, while the outer wall of the gate has animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.). In particular, cave 7 has eight triangular patterns arranged in a sawtooth pattern on the upper part of the outer wall, and in the upper right corner, a double triangular pattern is engraved on it, and on the right side, a kite-shaped quiver is drawn with ten arrows. Furthermore, five horses are engraved on the outer wall on the left, which is noteworthy for its dynamic and three-dimensional depiction. In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated [[grave goods]] include a straight sword and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. The site is about a five-minute walk from [[Hitoyoshi Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hisatsu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster |native_name =大村横穴群 |alternate_name = |image =Omura Yokoana Group, higashi-1.jpg |alt = |caption = Omura Yokoana Group | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates = {{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E|region:JP-45|display=inline}} | location = [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis | part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = |built = 6th-7th century CE |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster'''|大村横穴群|Ōmura yokoana-gun}} is a cluster of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs dug in artificial caves in a [[tuff]] cliff of located in the Shiromoto-machi neighborhood of the city of [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto|Hitoyoshi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139857 |title=大村横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Background== The Ōmura Cave Tombs are located over a 800-meter stretch from east-to-west on the cliffs on the south side of the Murayama Plateau on the right bank of the [[Kuma River (Japan)|Kuma River]], which runs through the south of the prefecture. These tombs were built during the Kofun period in the 6th and 7th centuries. The [[burial chamber]]s are square with rounded corners and two or three sections for the corpse bed. There are 27 tombs in the group, eight of which are [[decorated kofun]], with relief carings or paintings of animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.) on the outside. The entrance gates are square or dome-shaped, and many have decorative borders. The inner wall of the chamber of cave 15 has five double circular lines engraved on it, while the outer wall of the gate has animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.). In particular, cave 7 has eight triangular patterns arranged in a sawtooth pattern on the upper part of the outer wall, and in the upper right corner, a double triangular pattern is engraved on it, and on the right side, a kite-shaped quiver is drawn with ten arrows. Furthermore, five horses are engraved on the outer wall on the left, which is noteworthy for its dynamic and three-dimensional depiction. In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated [[grave goods]] include a straight sword and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. The site is about a five-minute walk from [[Hitoyoshi Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hisatsu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139857 |title=大村横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Background== The Ōmura Cave Tombs are located over a 800-meter stretch from east-to-west on the cliffs on the south side of the Murayama Plateau on the right bank of the [[Kuma River (Japan)|Kuma River]], which runs through the south of the prefecture. These tombs were built during the Kofun period in the 6th and 7th centuries. The [[burial chamber]]s are square with rounded corners and two or three sections for the corpse bed. There are 27 tombs in the group, eight of which are [[decorated kofun]], with relief carings or paintings of animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.) on the outside. The entrance gates are square or dome-shaped, and many have decorative borders. The inner wall of the chamber of cave 15 has five double circular lines engraved on it, while the outer wall of the gate has animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.). In particular, cave 7 has eight triangular patterns arranged in a sawtooth pattern on the upper part of the outer wall, and in the upper right corner, a double triangular pattern is engraved on it, and on the right side, a kite-shaped quiver is drawn with ten arrows. Furthermore, five horses are engraved on the outer wall on the left, which is noteworthy for its dynamic and three-dimensional depiction. In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster |native_name =大村横穴群 |alternate_name = |image =Omura Yokoana Group, higashi-1.jpg |alt = |caption = Omura Yokoana Group | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates = {{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E|region:JP-45|display=inline}} | location = [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis | part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = |built = 6th-7th century CE |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster'''|大村横穴群|Ōmura yokoana-gun}} is a cluster of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs dug in artificial caves in a [[tuff]] cliff of located in the Shiromoto-machi neighborhood of the city of [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto|Hitoyoshi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139857 |title=大村横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Background== The Ōmura Cave Tombs are located over a 800-meter stretch from east-to-west on the cliffs on the south side of the Murayama Plateau on the right bank of the [[Kuma River (Japan)|Kuma River]], which runs through the south of the prefecture. These tombs were built during the Kofun period in the 6th and 7th centuries. The [[burial chamber]]s are square with rounded corners and two or three sections for the corpse bed. There are 27 tombs in the group, eight of which are [[decorated kofun]], with relief carings or paintings of animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.) on the outside. The entrance gates are square or dome-shaped, and many have decorative borders. The inner wall of the chamber of cave 15 has five double circular lines engraved on it, while the outer wall of the gate has animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.). In particular, cave 7 has eight triangular patterns arranged in a sawtooth pattern on the upper part of the outer wall, and in the upper right corner, a double triangular pattern is engraved on it, and on the right side, a kite-shaped quiver is drawn with ten arrows. Furthermore, five horses are engraved on the outer wall on the left, which is noteworthy for its dynamic and three-dimensional depiction. In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated [[grave goods]] include a straight sword and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. The site is about a five-minute walk from [[Hitoyoshi Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hisatsu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Omura Yokoana Group}} *[https://hitoyoshikuma-guide.com/2019/03/03/oomurayokoanagun/ Hitoyoshi-Kuma Guide]{{in lang|ja}} Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Corridor-type kofun]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[Category:imported Kofun]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:cgoodbye $1 tier 5]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster]] [[en:Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster]] {{citation needed|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:New]]

The {{Nihongo|'''Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster'''|大村横穴群|Ōmura yokoana-gun}} is a cluster of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs dug in artificial caves in a [[tuff]] cliff of located in the Shiromoto-machi neighborhood of the city of [[Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto|Hitoyoshi]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139857 |title=大村横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Background== The Ōmura Cave Tombs are located over a 800-meter stretch from east-to-west on the cliffs on the south side of the Murayama Plateau on the right bank of the [[Kuma River (Japan)|Kuma River]], which runs through the south of the prefecture. These tombs were built during the Kofun period in the 6th and 7th centuries. The [[burial chamber]]s are square with rounded corners and two or three sections for the corpse bed. There are 27 tombs in the group, eight of which are [[decorated kofun]], with relief carings or paintings of animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.) on the outside. The entrance gates are square or dome-shaped, and many have decorative borders. The inner wall of the chamber of cave 15 has five double circular lines engraved on it, while the outer wall of the gate has animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.). In particular, cave 7 has eight triangular patterns arranged in a sawtooth pattern on the upper part of the outer wall, and in the upper right corner, a double triangular pattern is engraved on it, and on the right side, a kite-shaped quiver is drawn with ten arrows. Furthermore, five horses are engraved on the outer wall on the left, which is noteworthy for its dynamic and three-dimensional depiction. In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated [[grave goods]] include a straight sword and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. The site is about a five-minute walk from [[Hitoyoshi Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hisatsu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

==External links== {{Commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Omura Yokoana Group}} *[https://hitoyoshikuma-guide.com/2019/03/03/oomurayokoanagun/ Hitoyoshi-Kuma Guide]{{in lang|ja}} Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster

In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated [[grave goods]] include a straight sword and [[Sue pottery]] pottery. The site is about a five-minute walk from [[Hitoyoshi Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hisatsu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139857 |title=大村横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Background== The Ōmura Cave Tombs are located over a 800-meter stretch from east-to-west on the cliffs on the south side of the Murayama Plateau on the right bank of the [[:en:Kuma River (Japan)|Kuma River]], which runs through the south of the prefecture. These tombs were built during the Kofun period in the 6th and 7th centuries. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s are square with rounded corners and two or three sections for the corpse bed. There are 27 tombs in the group, eight of which are [[decorated kofun]], with relief carings or paintings of animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.) on the outside. The entrance gates are square or dome-shaped, and many have decorative borders. The inner wall of the chamber of cave 15 has five double circular lines engraved on it, while the outer wall of the gate has animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.). In particular, cave 7 has eight triangular patterns arranged in a sawtooth pattern on the upper part of the outer wall, and in the upper right corner, a double triangular pattern is engraved on it, and on the right side, a kite-shaped quiver is drawn with ten arrows. Furthermore, five horses are engraved on the outer wall on the left, which is noteworthy for its dynamic and three-dimensional depiction. In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include a straight sword and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. The site is about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Hitoyoshi Station|Hitoyoshi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Hisatsu Line|Hisatsu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omura Yokoana Group]][[en:Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster]][[ja:大村横穴群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q97220287]] Wikidata name: Ōmura Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster |native_name =大村横穴群 |alternate_name = |image =Omura Yokoana Group, higashi-1.jpg |alt = |caption = Omura Yokoana Group | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | map_caption = Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster | map_size = 270px |coordinates = {{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E|region:JP-45|display=inline}} | location = [[:en:Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto|Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = necropolis | part_of = |length = |width = |area = |height = |builder = |material = |built = 6th-7th century CE |abandoned = |epochs = [[Kofun period]] |cultures = |dependency_of = |occupants = |event = |excavations = |archaeologists = |condition = |ownership = Public |management = |public_access = Yes |website = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Special Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|13|01|N|130|45|15|E}}}}}}
The {{Nihongo|'''Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster'''|大村横穴群|Ōmura yokoana-gun}} is a cluster of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs dug in artificial caves in a [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] cliff of located in the Shiromoto-machi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto|Hitoyoshi]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139857 |title=大村横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Background== The Ōmura Cave Tombs are located over a 800-meter stretch from east-to-west on the cliffs on the south side of the Murayama Plateau on the right bank of the [[:en:Kuma River (Japan)|Kuma River]], which runs through the south of the prefecture. These tombs were built during the Kofun period in the 6th and 7th centuries. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s are square with rounded corners and two or three sections for the corpse bed. There are 27 tombs in the group, eight of which are [[decorated kofun]], with relief carings or paintings of animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.) on the outside. The entrance gates are square or dome-shaped, and many have decorative borders. The inner wall of the chamber of cave 15 has five double circular lines engraved on it, while the outer wall of the gate has animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.). In particular, cave 7 has eight triangular patterns arranged in a sawtooth pattern on the upper part of the outer wall, and in the upper right corner, a double triangular pattern is engraved on it, and on the right side, a kite-shaped quiver is drawn with ten arrows. Furthermore, five horses are engraved on the outer wall on the left, which is noteworthy for its dynamic and three-dimensional depiction. In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include a straight sword and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. The site is about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Hitoyoshi Station|Hitoyoshi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Hisatsu Line|Hisatsu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

The {{Nihongo|'''Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster'''|大村横穴群|Ōmura yokoana-gun}} is a cluster of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs dug in artificial caves in a [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] cliff of located in the Shiromoto-machi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto|Hitoyoshi]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139857 |title=大村横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>

These tombs were built during the Kofun period in the 6th and 7th centuries. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s are square with rounded corners and two or three sections for the corpse bed. There are 27 tombs in the group, eight of which are [[decorated kofun]], with relief carings or paintings of animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.) on the outside. The entrance gates are square or dome-shaped, and many have decorative borders. The inner wall of the chamber of cave 15 has five double circular lines engraved on it, while the outer wall of the gate has animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.). In particular, cave 7 has eight triangular patterns arranged in a sawtooth pattern on the upper part of the outer wall, and in the upper right corner, a double triangular pattern is engraved on it, and on the right side, a kite-shaped quiver is drawn with ten arrows. Furthermore, five horses are engraved on the outer wall on the left, which is noteworthy for its dynamic and three-dimensional depiction. In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The {{Nihongo|'''Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster'''|大村横穴群|Ōmura yokoana-gun}} is a cluster of {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} tombs dug in artificial caves in a [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] cliff of located in the Shiromoto-machi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Hitoyoshi, Kumamoto|Hitoyoshi]], [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]] in [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1921.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139857 |title=大村横穴群 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Background== The Ōmura Cave Tombs are located over a 800-meter stretch from east-to-west on the cliffs on the south side of the Murayama Plateau on the right bank of the [[:en:Kuma River (Japan)|Kuma River]], which runs through the south of the prefecture. These tombs were built during the Kofun period in the 6th and 7th centuries. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s are square with rounded corners and two or three sections for the corpse bed. There are 27 tombs in the group, eight of which are [[decorated kofun]], with relief carings or paintings of animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.) on the outside. The entrance gates are square or dome-shaped, and many have decorative borders. The inner wall of the chamber of cave 15 has five double circular lines engraved on it, while the outer wall of the gate has animals, weapons, armor, and geometric patterns (circles, triangles, etc.). In particular, cave 7 has eight triangular patterns arranged in a sawtooth pattern on the upper part of the outer wall, and in the upper right corner, a double triangular pattern is engraved on it, and on the right side, a kite-shaped quiver is drawn with ten arrows. Furthermore, five horses are engraved on the outer wall on the left, which is noteworthy for its dynamic and three-dimensional depiction. In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include a straight sword and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. The site is about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Hitoyoshi Station|Hitoyoshi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Hisatsu Line|Hisatsu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

In particular, cave 7 has eight triangular patterns arranged in a sawtooth pattern on the upper part of the outer wall, and in the upper right corner, a double triangular pattern is engraved on it, and on the right side, a kite-shaped quiver is drawn with ten arrows. Furthermore, five horses are engraved on the outer wall on the left, which is noteworthy for its dynamic and three-dimensional depiction. In addition, in the No. 11 cave, two quivers, five circular patterns, and a knife are painted on the left side of the outer wall, and one quiver is painted on the right side. In the No. 14 cave, a wheel shape and a shield are painted on the top and two quivers are painted on the left.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include a straight sword and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. The site is about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Hitoyoshi Station|Hitoyoshi Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Hisatsu Line|Hisatsu Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
